---
title: "quiz10"
title-block-style: none
format: live-html
engine: knitr
sidebar:
  style: docked
resources:
  -  marketing_A.csv
  -  marketing_B.csv
---

{{< include /_extensions/r-wasm/live/_knitr.qmd >}}
{{< include /_extensions/r-wasm/live/_gradethis.qmd >}}

<script>
document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", () => {
  const params = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);
  const showQ = params.get("q");

  // Show only the requested section
  if (showQ) {
    document.querySelectorAll("section[id]").forEach(section => {
      if (section.id !== showQ) section.style.display = "none";
    });
  }

  // Hide page title
  document.querySelectorAll(".quarto-title-block")
    .forEach(el => el.style.display = "none");

  // Hide ALL section titles (## ...)
  document.querySelectorAll("section[id] > h2")
    .forEach(h => h.style.display = "none");
});
</script>

## Q1 — Visualize the Relationship {#q1a}
Using the `marketing_A.csv` dataset:

Does the plot suggest a linear relationship?
```{webr}
quiz <- read.csv("marketing_A.csv") 
plot(quiz$ad_spend, quiz$sales,
     xlab = "Ad Spend ($)", ylab = "Sales ($)")
abline(lm(sales ~ ad_spend, data = quiz), col = "blue")
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false

# Yes
```

## Q1 — Visualize the Relationship {#q1b}
Using the `marketing_B.csv` dataset:

Does the plot suggest a linear relationship?
```{webr}
quiz <- read.csv("marketing_B.csv") 
plot(quiz$ad_spend, quiz$sales,
     xlab = "Ad Spend ($)", ylab = "Sales ($)")
abline(lm(sales ~ ad_spend, data = quiz), col = "blue")
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false

# Yes
```

## Q2 — Fit the Regression Model {#q2a}

Using the `marketing_A.csv` dataset:

Fit a simple linear regression model with `sales` as the response and `ad_spend` as the predictor.

Return the estimated slope.

```{webr}
quiz <- read.csv("marketing_A.csv")
model <- lm(___ ~ ___, data = quiz)
coef(model)
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
quiz <- read.csv("marketing_A.csv")
model <- lm(sales ~ ad_spend, data = quiz)
coef(model)
# 2.5406
```

## Q2 — Fit the Regression Model {#q2b}

Using the `marketing_B.csv` dataset:

Fit a simple linear regression model with `sales` as the response and `ad_spend` as the predictor.

Return the estimated slope.

```{webr}
quiz <- read.csv("marketing_B.csv")
model <- lm(___ ~ ___, data = quiz)
coef(model)
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
quiz <- read.csv("marketing_B.csv")
model <- lm(sales ~ ad_spend, data = quiz)
coef(model)
# 2.2406
```
## Q3 — Compute R² {#q3a}

Using the `marketing_A.csv` dataset:

Return the model’s $R^2$.
```{webr}
quiz <- read.csv("marketing_A.csv")
model <- lm(___ ~ ___, data = quiz)
summary(model)
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
quiz <- read.csv("marketing_A.csv")
model <- lm(sales ~ ad_spend, data = quiz)
summary(model)
# 0.8827 
```
## Q3 — Compute R² {#q3b}

Using the `marketing_B.csv` dataset:

Return the model’s $R^2$.
```{webr}
quiz <- read.csv("marketing_B.csv")
model <- lm(___ ~ ___, data = quiz)
summary(model)
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
quiz <- read.csv("marketing_B.csv")
model <- lm(sales ~ ad_spend, data = quiz)
summary(model)
# 0.8540
```
## Q4 — Compute the p-value for the slope {#q4a}

Using the `marketing_A.csv` dataset:

Compute the p-value for testing $H_0: \beta_1 = 0$.

```{webr}
quiz <- read.csv("marketing_A.csv")
model <- lm(___ ~ ___, data = quiz)
summary(model)
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
quiz <- read.csv("marketing_A.csv")
model <- lm(sales ~ ad_spend, data = quiz)
summary(model)
# 1.484e-14
```

## Q4 — Compute the p-value for the slope {#q4b}

Using the `marketing_B.csv` dataset:

Compute the p-value for testing $H_0: \beta_1 = 0$.

```{webr}
quiz <- read.csv("marketing_B.csv")
model <- lm(___ ~ ___, data = quiz)
summary(model)
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
quiz <- read.csv("marketing_B.csv")
model <- lm(sales ~ ad_spend, data = quiz)
summary(model)
# 3.204e-13
```

## Q5 — Prediction (Interpolation) {#q5a}

Using the `marketing_A.csv` dataset:

Predict the mean `sales` when `ad_spend` = 6000.
Round to the nearest integer.

```{webr}
quiz <- read.csv("marketing_A.csv")
model <- lm(___ ~ ___, data = quiz)
predict(___, newdata = data.frame(ad_spend = ___))
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
quiz <- read.csv("marketing_A.csv")
model <- lm(sales ~ ad_spend, data = quiz)
round(predict(model, newdata = data.frame(ad_spend = 6000)))
# 20901 
```

## Q5 — Prediction (Interpolation) {#q5b}

Using the `marketing_B.csv` dataset:

Predict the mean `sales` when `ad_spend` = 6000.
Round to the nearest integer.

```{webr}
quiz <- read.csv("marketing_B.csv")
model <- lm(___ ~ ___, data = quiz)
predict(___, newdata = data.frame(ad_spend = ___))
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
quiz <- read.csv("marketing_B.csv")
model <- lm(sales ~ ad_spend, data = quiz)
round(predict(model, newdata = data.frame(ad_spend = 6000)))
# 19101
```

##  Q6 — Interpolation Reliability {#q6a}

For the prediction at `ad_spend` = 6000:

Is this prediction reliable?

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false

#Yes

```

##  Q6 — Interpolation Reliability {#q6b}

For the prediction at `ad_spend` = 6000:

Is this prediction reliable?

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false

#Yes

```
## Q7 — Prediction (Extrapolation) {#q7a}

Using the `marketing_A.csv` dataset:

Predict the mean `sales` when `ad_spend` = 20000.
(*Round to the nearest integer*.)
```{webr}
quiz <- read.csv("marketing_A.csv")
model <- lm(___ ~ ___, data = quiz)
predict(___, newdata = data.frame(ad_spend = ___))
```


```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
quiz <- read.csv("marketing_A.csv")

model <- lm(sales ~ ad_spend, data = quiz)
predict(model, newdata = data.frame(ad_spend = 20000))
#56469
```

## Q7 — Prediction (Extrapolation) {#q7b}

Using the `marketing_B.csv` dataset:

Predict the mean `sales` when `ad_spend` = 20000.
(*Round to the nearest integer*.)
```{webr}
quiz <- read.csv("marketing_B.csv")
model <- lm(___ ~ ___, data = quiz)
predict(___, newdata = data.frame(ad_spend = ___))
```


```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
quiz <- read.csv("marketing_B.csv")

model <- lm(sales ~ ad_spend, data = quiz)
predict(model, newdata = data.frame(ad_spend = 20000))
#50469
```


## Q8 — Extrapolation Reliability {#q8a}

For the prediction at `ad_spend` = 20000:

Is this prediction reliable?

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false

#No

```

## Q8 — Extrapolation Reliability {#q8b}

For the prediction at `ad_spend` = 20000:

Is this prediction reliable?

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false

#No

```

## Q9 — Residual Plot {#q9a}

Using the `marketing_A.csv` dataset, fit a simple linear regression model with `sales` as the response and `ad_spend` as the predictor.

Construct a residual plot of the fitted values versus residuals.

Select ALL features that indicate a good linear regression model:

```{webr}
quiz <- read.csv("marketing_A.csv")

model <- lm(___ ~ ___, data = quiz)

plot(___, ___,
     xlab = "Fitted Values",
     ylab = "Residuals")

abline(h = 0)
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
quiz <- read.csv("marketing_A.csv")

model <- lm(sales ~ ad_spend, data = quiz)

plot(model$fitted.values, model$residuals,
     xlab = "Fitted Values",
     ylab = "Residuals")

abline(h = 0)

# Random scatter around 0 
# No clear pattern
# Points spread out more as values increase
# Curved pattern

# correct answers: Random scatter around 0, and No clear pattern
```

## Q9 — Residual Plot {#q9b}

Using the `marketing_B.csv` dataset, fit a simple linear regression model with `sales` as the response and `ad_spend` as the predictor.

Construct a residual plot of the fitted values versus residuals.

Select ALL features that indicate a good linear regression model:

```{webr}
quiz <- read.csv("marketing_B.csv")

model <- lm(___ ~ ___, data = quiz)

plot(___, ___,
     xlab = "Fitted Values",
     ylab = "Residuals")

abline(h = 0)
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
quiz <- read.csv("marketing_B.csv")

model <- lm(sales ~ ad_spend, data = quiz)

plot(model$fitted.values, model$residuals,
     xlab = "Fitted Values",
     ylab = "Residuals")

abline(h = 0)

# Random scatter around 0
# No clear pattern
# Points spread out more as values increase
# Curved pattern

# correct answers: Random scatter around 0, and No clear pattern
```
